meta.title: carl — Roadmap
meta.blurb: A living plan for the game. We edit roadmap.json together: flip statuses, add items, revise decisions as we learn from playing. Keep it honest — if something's half-done or unproven, say so.
meta.vision: A 3D action game with boss fights, fighting through levels, and puzzles. Built in Godot 4. Currently a prototype — proving the core loop is fun before scaling up.
meta.note: Everything is placeholder primitives (capsules/boxes) — no art committed yet.

sections.title: Completed
sections.desc: Shipped chunks of work, newest first. Each group maps to one commit.

sections.title: Ideas & open questions
sections.desc: Directions under consideration, not yet committed.

sections.title: Known issues / debt
sections.desc: Things that work but aren't right yet.

decisions.text: 3D isometric first — to test the most ambitious vision before simplifying.
Combat feel decided by playing — hence the combat_mode switch in combat.gd. Currently ranged.
First-person added as a toggle, not a replacement — so we can A/B without losing the iso work.
Art deferred — placeholder shapes until the loop is proven fun.
Considering: view-switching as a core mechanic — keep both iso and FP and design around the swap, rather than picking one. Not yet committed.

items.title: Interactive HTML roadmap
children: Roadmap moved from markdown to a JSON-backed HTML page (roadmap/index.html + roadmap.json)
Dashboard stats, search, status filters, collapsible sections, hash deep-links, starred-idea highlight
ROADMAP.md retired to a pointer; .claude/ gitignored

items.title: Weapons & FPS-style shooting
children: Body gun (visible iso/third-person) + first-person viewmodel (bottom-right, FP-only)
Projectiles spawn from the active muzzle; FP shots follow the camera's full 3D aim (incl. pitch), iso stays flat

items.title: First-person mode + presentation polish
children: First-person view toggle (V): mouse-look, camera-relative movement, camera-forward aim
FP crosshair (HUD, FP-only); fixed the HUD swallowing mouse-look input
Lit environment (sky + ambient) so both iso and FP read clearly
Per-instance enemy/boss materials (fixed all enemies flashing together)

items.title: Level, platforming & enemies
children: One linear level: start → path → arena → platforming → arena → sealed boss arena
Jump + gravity + coyote time; platforming over pits; landing marker (iso) to judge jumps
Fall → checkpoint respawn with fall damage; checkpoints at each arena
Small chaser enemies (detect → chase → contact damage)
Boss: dormant until you enter its arena, then CHASE → TELEGRAPH → ATTACK → COOLDOWN
Follow camera with look-ahead (iso)

items.title: Core combat prototype
children: Iso movement (camera-relative WASD) + mouse-cursor aiming
Combat system with a one-switch feel selector (combat_mode: melee / ranged / mixed) — currently ranged
Reliable projectile hits (stepped sphere-overlap; no tunnelling/vertical misses)
HUD: player + boss HP bars, win/lose message
Reusable health.gd shared by player/enemies/boss

items.title: First-person feel & weapon polish
items.note: the FP gun works but feels flat
children: Muzzle flash + a tracer/visible-bullet read on firing
Recoil / weapon-sway / fire kick so shots have weight
Replace the blocky placeholder gun shapes with something more gun-like
Crosshair could tighten/tint when on-target
Mouse-look tuning — sensitivity, optional invert-Y, adjustable FOV

items.title: Enemy variety
items.note: at least one ranged/turret enemy so fights aren't all melee-chasers

items.title: Combat feedback
items.note: hit sound/particle, damage numbers or screen-shake, death effect polish

items.title: Boss fight depth
items.note: more than one attack; a second phase at low HP

items.title: A real puzzle
items.note: the pressure-plate/door probe exists; build one actual puzzle into the level

items.title: Player feedback when hit
items.note: screen flash / knockback (right now only the HP bar moves)

items.title: Main menu + pause + proper game-over/restart flow
Art pass
items.note: commit to a style once the loop is locked

items.title: View-switching as a core mechanic
items.note: The standout idea: instead of choosing iso OR first-person, the player switches between them on purpose (V key), and the game is designed around that switch. Each view reveals and hides different things, so the swap becomes a verb. Promising because both views already work and share all combat/enemy/level logic — the switch is real today.
children: What does each view give you? iso = whole-arena read, enemy positions, platform layout, incoming attacks; FP = precise aiming, see far down corridors, spot things hidden from the top-down angle. Only interesting if each view has a real advantage.
Puzzles/level design that REQUIRE switching — a gap only judgeable in iso, a distant target only hittable in FP, a path visible from only one angle.
Is the switch free or costed? Instant, cooldown, limited charges, or a resource? A cost makes 'when do I switch?' a decision.
Combat framing — does aiming/feel differ enough to switch mid-fight (FP to snipe, iso to kite a swarm)?
Readability & comfort — sudden iso↔FP swaps can disorient; maybe a quick transition tween rather than a hard cut.
Next concrete step: prototype one encounter only solvable by switching views, and see if the switch feels like a tool or a chore.

items.title: The camera question (single view vs dual-view)
items.note: Originally 'pick one view and delete the other.' Now it's pick-one OR commit to the dual-view-as-mechanic idea above.
children: Play both modes through the whole level and note how each feels
Decide the direction: single view (FP / iso / over-shoulder) or dual-view-as-mechanic
Once decided, design encounters/level geometry for it (sightlines differ a lot between views)

items.title: Level geometry was laid out for the iso read; it works in FP but isn't designed for it.
No crosshair/aim-assist read in iso beyond the character facing; fine for now.
combat.gd MIXED mode is a quick 'both at once' — revisit if mixed becomes the direction.
Restart is just R (reloads the scene); no UI around death/victory yet.
